title: InDex: Bridging the Morphology Gap: Adapting VLA Models to Dexterous Manipulation via Intent-Conditioned Fine-Tuning
summary: InDex is a framework for adapting Vision-Language-Action models trained on simple parallel grippers to complex multi-fingered dexterous hands. Direct end-to-end joint fine-tuning causes catastrophic forgetting of spatial reasoning and action collapse under limited data. InDex uses a two-stage approach in which parallel-gripper outputs serve as intent proxies, enabling efficient dexterous skill learning while preserving the original model's spatial generalization capabilities.
